The repository search results indicate that the channels configuration is defined in `.gitclaw/channels/routes.yaml`. The report in the issue thread references the `telegram` channel and a thread with ID `gitclaw-e2e-telegram-{route}-{message_id}`. The report is part of an end-to-end (E2E) test for channels, specifically for the date 20260603T204108Z. The corresponding token in the search results for configuration phrase "config." in context is:

which confirms channel bridge config validation is associated with `.gitclaw/config.yml`.
